column1: After a meal like that, you feel it will be a struggle even to make it to bed.  But, what the hey, you're a Hero, you can do it!
column2: After a meal like that, you feel it will be a struggle even to make it to bed.  But, what the hey, you're a Hero, you can do it!

column1: You should order a meal first.  It would be rude to eat your travel rations in your friends' Inn.
column2: You should order a meal first.  It would be rude to eat your travel rations in your friends' Inn.

column1: You have no food.
column2: You have no food.

column1: Hero-friend, a most strange fire has been seen outside the Inn.  Take care as you leave.
column2: Hero-friend, a most strange fire has been seen outside the Inn.  Take care as you leave.

column1: The poet has personally asked that you be present tonight, for he wishes to speak with you after his reading.
column2: The poet has personally asked that you be present tonight, for he wishes to speak with you after his reading.

column1: The poet will soon speak, if you will but be seated.
column2: The poet will soon speak, if you will but be seated.

column1: Did you have a chance to meet and speak with those in our fair bazaar?  It is said there are many rumors in this city (but don't quote me on that).
column2: Did you have a chance to meet and speak with those in our fair bazaar?  It is said there are many rumors in this city (but don't quote me on that).

column1: I am here to serve you, Hero.
column2: I am here to serve you, Hero.

column1: Here is your tea.
column2: Here is your tea.

column1: The Poet Omar will thrill all who hear with his words of song after the setting of the sun, in our own Inn!
column2: The Poet Omar will thrill all who hear with his words of song after the setting of the sun, in our own Inn!

column1: We are most honored by your presence at our Inn, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: We are most honored by your presence at our Inn, Hero of Spielburg.

column1: Thank you, they are most beautiful.
column2: Thank you, they are most beautiful.

column1: It is always a pleasure to see you, Great Hero.
column2: It is always a pleasure to see you, Great Hero.

column1: You awaken much refreshed.
column2: You awaken much refreshed.

column1: I am so sorry that I have no answer to your question.
column2: I am so sorry that I have no answer to your question.

column1: It is good to be here again.  You will always be welcome in our Inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.
column2: It is good to be here again.  You will always be welcome in our Inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.

column1: All Kattas in that city were driven out of their homes there.  Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave.  I fear for him.
column2: All Kattas in that city were driven out of their homes there.  Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave.  I fear for him.

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our Inn.  You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our Inn.  You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.

column1: What better job for a Katta then one where you sit all day in the warm sun and chatter with friends?
column2: What better job for a Katta then one where you sit all day in the warm sun and chatter with friends?

column1: My name, in the language of our people, means "She of the Dance."
column2: My name, in the language of our people, means "She of the Dance."

column1: His name means "Honor".  He is very brave.  It is said that he refused to leave Raseir so that he could work to bring freedom to his homeland again.
column2: His name means "Honor".  He is very brave.  It is said that he refused to leave Raseir so that he could work to bring freedom to his homeland again.

column1: All Kattas are related in some manner to one another.
column2: All Kattas are related in some manner to one another.

column1: The dance that I perform was, at one time, to ready our people for battle, and...ah...for fertility.  Now it is only for fun.
column2: The dance that I perform was, at one time, to ready our people for battle, and...ah...for fertility.  Now it is only for fun.

column1: He is both my husband and my soul-mate.
column2: He is both my husband and my soul-mate.

column1: If the new Emir hates Kattas so, Sharaf could be in deadly trouble.
column2: If the new Emir hates Kattas so, Sharaf could be in deadly trouble.

column1: Lamb Felafels and soured cream, along with a rabbit curry, are part of the meal for tonight.  May it do you good.
column2: Lamb Felafels and soured cream, along with a rabbit curry, are part of the meal for tonight.  May it do you good.

column1: Y'get hash tonight, Mudball!  The designers didn't count on you eatin' on Sundays!
column2: Y'get hash tonight, Mudball!  The designers didn't count on you eatin' on Sundays!

column1: ack!
column2: ack!

column1: I beg you to be careful when you leave the Inn today.  The thing of fire grows ever more dangerous.
column2: I beg you to be careful when you leave the Inn today.  The thing of fire grows ever more dangerous.

column1: My second cousin Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace, and my third cousin once removed, Tiram the Rug Merchant, were unable to set up their stands today.  An Elemental of Air is filling the plaza!
column2: My second cousin Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace, and my third cousin once removed, Tiram the Rug Merchant, were unable to set up their stands today.  An Elemental of Air is filling the plaza!

column1: The Katta wish to thank you for your bravery and kindness.  Please go visit Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace today.
column2: The Katta wish to thank you for your bravery and kindness.  Please go visit Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace today.

column1: The Sultan Harun Al-Rashid welcomed and sheltered the Kattas driven from their homes in Raseir.  His name will be honored forever by our people.
column2: The Sultan Harun Al-Rashid welcomed and sheltered the Kattas driven from their homes in Raseir.  His name will be honored forever by our people.

column1: Those driven from Raseir speak highly of the former Emir Arus Al-din. Of his brother, Ali Hasan, they merely spit.
column2: Those driven from Raseir speak highly of the former Emir Arus Al-din. Of his brother, Ali Hasan, they merely spit.

column1: You have no need for your money in our Inn.  You will need to see the Money Changer, however, before you can buy something in Shapeir.
column2: You have no need for your money in our Inn.  You will need to see the Money Changer, however, before you can buy something in Shapeir.

column1: This is truly what an Inn should be, the center of all.  With Shema to dance, and the Poet Omar to entertain, there will always be something of interest here.
column2: This is truly what an Inn should be, the center of all.  With Shema to dance, and the Poet Omar to entertain, there will always be something of interest here.

column1: The pathway to the Fountain is North of our Plaza.
column2: The pathway to the Fountain is North of our Plaza.

column1: The Money Changer will change the money of Spielburg for the coins of Shapeir.
column2: The Money Changer will change the money of Spielburg for the coins of Shapeir.

column1: The Kattas believe one's fate lies within oneself, not in the stars above.
column2: The Kattas believe one's fate lies within oneself, not in the stars above.

column1: Poetry is revered in this land.  The words of the poet Omar speak both to the heart and to the mind.
column2: Poetry is revered in this land.  The words of the poet Omar speak both to the heart and to the mind.

column1: Kattas are fine sellers of many things, for they know the true value of wealth.
column2: Kattas are fine sellers of many things, for they know the true value of wealth.

column1: Shameen, in the tongue of my kindred, means, "He of the Lion."
column2: Shameen, in the tongue of my kindred, means, "He of the Lion."

column1: Kattas feel you should go where your heart leads you, and there you will find yourself.
column2: Kattas feel you should go where your heart leads you, and there you will find yourself.

column1: We journeyed to Spielburg to bring back a Hero.
column2: We journeyed to Spielburg to bring back a Hero.

column1: We are kin, the Liontaur race and the Kattas.
column2: We are kin, the Liontaur race and the Kattas.

column1: In the morning and evening, you have but to sit at your table and Shema will be honored to serve you.  You are our guest.
column2: In the morning and evening, you have but to sit at your table and Shema will be honored to serve you.  You are our guest.

column1: Because you defeated the Brigands in Spielburg and returned our stolen treasure, we shall be forever in your debt.  Joining us on our journey to Shapeir and helping us set up this Inn has made you our friend, as well.
column2: Because you defeated the Brigands in Spielburg and returned our stolen treasure, we shall be forever in your debt.  Joining us on our journey to Shapeir and helping us set up this Inn has made you our friend, as well.

column1: The land of Shapeir may be plagued with Elementals and Djinns, but this city will ever stand, as long as there are heroes such as yourself.
column2: The land of Shapeir may be plagued with Elementals and Djinns, but this city will ever stand, as long as there are heroes such as yourself.

column1: Raseir and Shapeir were sister cities, separated by the sands of the desert.  Now it is said that the fountain of Raseir runs no more, and that the city's soul has shriveled like a grape in the sun.
column2: Raseir and Shapeir were sister cities, separated by the sands of the desert.  Now it is said that the fountain of Raseir runs no more, and that the city's soul has shriveled like a grape in the sun.

column1: A Water Elemental in the Fountain of town would be the greatest of misfortunes.
column2: A Water Elemental in the Fountain of town would be the greatest of misfortunes.

column1: It is said to be a living Whirlwind.
column2: It is said to be a living Whirlwind.

column1: It is rock and stone molded into a living creature with all the destructive power of the Earthquake.
column2: It is rock and stone molded into a living creature with all the destructive power of the Earthquake.

column1: The other day when I was talking to the Sultan (May-He-Live-Forever!), as Harun Al-Rashid thanked me for my gift of the flying carpet and complimented me upon my courage in fighting against those vile brigands of Spielburg, he mentioned that there might be a need of a hero around here soon.  The Sultan must have talked to the Astrologer.
column2: The other day when I was talking to the Sultan (May-He-Live-Forever!), as Harun Al-Rashid thanked me for my gift of the flying carpet and complimented me upon my courage in fighting against those vile brigands of Spielburg, he mentioned that there might be a need of a hero around here soon.  The Sultan must have talked to the Astrologer.

column1: You hope to come to WIT's end, ha ha!  I have heard rumors that the Wizard's school is around here, but I, who have lived my life here, have seen nothing of it.
column2: You hope to come to WIT's end, ha ha!  I have heard rumors that the Wizard's school is around here, but I, who have lived my life here, have seen nothing of it.

column1: When it comes to money, I am the person to ask!  You will need to find the Money Changer to trade over your coins before you can buy anything here.
column2: When it comes to money, I am the person to ask!  You will need to find the Money Changer to trade over your coins before you can buy anything here.

column1: It has been a long time since I have visited there, but I know this city like the back of my hand.  It is to the east of us, off the Plaza of the Fighters.  Or was that the west?
column2: It has been a long time since I have visited there, but I know this city like the back of my hand.  It is to the east of us, off the Plaza of the Fighters.  Or was that the west?

column1: Thieves' Guild?  What need have you for thieves?  There is no such guild in this fair city!
column2: Thieves' Guild?  What need have you for thieves?  There is no such guild in this fair city!

column1: In the day, it is hot...at night, it is cold.  It is always the way this time of year.
column2: In the day, it is hot...at night, it is cold.  It is always the way this time of year.

column1: She is a very beautiful woman, and very attracted to me, I can tell.  You will find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, a street to the...hum...I think to the west of here.
column2: She is a very beautiful woman, and very attracted to me, I can tell.  You will find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, a street to the...hum...I think to the west of here.

column1: The Astrologer is a very important man here.  He can see the future in the stars!  You should go to him and find out your fortune, for is it not written, "A man should know his fate"?  His house is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: The Astrologer is a very important man here.  He can see the future in the stars!  You should go to him and find out your fortune, for is it not written, "A man should know his fate"?  His house is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.

column1: It is said, "The Poet sees the soul and lets it fly."  I, too, was a poet in my youth.
column2: It is said, "The Poet sees the soul and lets it fly."  I, too, was a poet in my youth.

column1: The Kattas make great neighbors and friends, and Shameen and Shema are the finest of Kattas!
column2: The Kattas make great neighbors and friends, and Shameen and Shema are the finest of Kattas!

column1: He lives in the desert by the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.
column2: He lives in the desert by the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.

column1: Like a cat, Shameen lands upon his paws where ever he goes.  This Inn is the Oasis of Culture for Shapeir.
column2: Like a cat, Shameen lands upon his paws where ever he goes.  This Inn is the Oasis of Culture for Shapeir.

column1: Shema's cooking is fantastic and her tea is heavenly.  Truly the Kattas are friends beyond measure.
column2: Shema's cooking is fantastic and her tea is heavenly.  Truly the Kattas are friends beyond measure.

column1: It seems my friend Arus Al-Din has been missing for almost a year now.
column2: It seems my friend Arus Al-Din has been missing for almost a year now.

column1: Magic is very much a part of life in shapeir.
column2: Magic is very much a part of life in shapeir.

column1: Upon that question     I would further ponder,  for guidance I suggest     that thee seek yonder.
column2: Upon that question     I would further ponder,  for guidance I suggest     that thee seek yonder.

column1: The poet Omar (May His Words Be Ever Heard!) has no answer for you.
column2: The poet Omar (May His Words Be Ever Heard!) has no answer for you.

column1: Upon some things     I can but little say,  that others yet might     further guide your way.
column2: Upon some things     I can but little say,  that others yet might     further guide your way.

column1: Companion, conscience,     ready to defend,  Teller to those that     they may comprehend,  A man of pride,     yet sits he by my side,  A person I am pleased     to call my friend.
column2: Companion, conscience,     ready to defend,  Teller to those that     they may comprehend,  A man of pride,     yet sits he by my side,  A person I am pleased     to call my friend.

column1: Rasha Rakeesh Sah Tarna,     it has been said,  Is a lion in the soul     as in the head.  Denying glory,     walks where heart has led,  A ruler who now     rules himself instead.
column2: Rasha Rakeesh Sah Tarna,     it has been said,  Is a lion in the soul     as in the head.  Denying glory,     walks where heart has led,  A ruler who now     rules himself instead.

column1: A Paladin travels     where his conscience leads,  Quietly helping others     with their needs.  Not for glory,     neither then for gain,  Good alone shall be     the judge he heeds.
column2: A Paladin travels     where his conscience leads,  Quietly helping others     with their needs.  Not for glory,     neither then for gain,  Good alone shall be     the judge he heeds.

column1: A Paladin goes wherever there is need, and obeys only true Justice and Goodness.
column2: A Paladin goes wherever there is need, and obeys only true Justice and Goodness.

column1: By actions rash,     a person might then find,  That others take exceptions     to his crime.  And rudeness returned     rudely thus remind,  That person to return     to things sublime.
column2: By actions rash,     a person might then find,  That others take exceptions     to his crime.  And rudeness returned     rudely thus remind,  That person to return     to things sublime.

column1: It would be best if a person would sit down, that the poet Omar might read and illuminate the minds of all who hear.
column2: It would be best if a person would sit down, that the poet Omar might read and illuminate the minds of all who hear.

column1: If it would please     the Hero so,  Then there is that     which he should know.  A simple prize     from Sultan wise,  For deeds of Air,     the land does owe.
column2: If it would please     the Hero so,  Then there is that     which he should know.  A simple prize     from Sultan wise,  For deeds of Air,     the land does owe.

column1: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elemental of Air.
column2: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elemental of Air.

column1: My thanks that you returned my purse,  Although it seems that it is much the worse  For a thief has stolen what was there  And gives me back a purse that now is bare.
column2: My thanks that you returned my purse,  Although it seems that it is much the worse  For a thief has stolen what was there  And gives me back a purse that now is bare.

column1: The poet is bothered more by the thief that by the loss of money.
column2: The poet is bothered more by the thief that by the loss of money.

column1: The Sultan of Shapeir     has now decreed,  That this hero is a Hero     here indeed.  For actions fundamental     in dispelling elementals,  A reward for he who freed     us with all speed.
column2: The Sultan of Shapeir     has now decreed,  That this hero is a Hero     here indeed.  For actions fundamental     in dispelling elementals,  A reward for he who freed     us with all speed.

column1: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elementals of Earth and Water.
column2: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elementals of Earth and Water.

column1: That which I lost     is now returned,  I give you the praise     of gratitude earned.
column2: That which I lost     is now returned,  I give you the praise     of gratitude earned.

column1: You may ask, "What is a Hero?"  Yet the answer's very clear,  He's the one that faces fear  When the Darkness gathers near.
column2: You may ask, "What is a Hero?"  Yet the answer's very clear,  He's the one that faces fear  When the Darkness gathers near.

column1: A Hero will brave the very Darkness for the needs of others.
column2: A Hero will brave the very Darkness for the needs of others.

column1: The Darkness gathers, and a man then strives     To do evil to another - Darkness thrives  The Darkness smiles, and those it gathers fight  Delighting Darkness as it takes their lives.
column2: The Darkness gathers, and a man then strives     To do evil to another - Darkness thrives  The Darkness smiles, and those it gathers fight  Delighting Darkness as it takes their lives.

column1: You are most welcome,     Hero who came here,  May you always be so     welcome in Shapeir.
column2: You are most welcome,     Hero who came here,  May you always be so     welcome in Shapeir.

column1: From the mountains whose tips     touch the stars,  To the city streets     filled with bazaars,  To the deserts of     flowing golden sand,  A tapestry of art     across the land.
column2: From the mountains whose tips     touch the stars,  To the city streets     filled with bazaars,  To the deserts of     flowing golden sand,  A tapestry of art     across the land.

column1: The Dark has touched her,     and has turned aside,  All things of light that     once did there abide.  Raseir is left to crumble     in despair,  Thus wickedness and folly     now reside.
column2: The Dark has touched her,     and has turned aside,  All things of light that     once did there abide.  Raseir is left to crumble     in despair,  Thus wickedness and folly     now reside.

column1: But in the Doer's     arrogance and pride,  The light grows ever stronger     now outside,  A Hero strives to force     the darkness back,  And into morning shall    be Raseir's guide.
column2: But in the Doer's     arrogance and pride,  The light grows ever stronger     now outside,  A Hero strives to force     the darkness back,  And into morning shall    be Raseir's guide.

column1: Mighty beings are     the magic Djinn  Who set the sands in motion     as the wind.  Who as the fire flames     the earth within  In the desert dance     undisciplined.
column2: Mighty beings are     the magic Djinn  Who set the sands in motion     as the wind.  Who as the fire flames     the earth within  In the desert dance     undisciplined.

column1: The Fire Elemental,     living flame,  Destruction unleashed,     wild and never tame.  Causing all it touches     then to burn,  Leaving only ashes     in return.
column2: The Fire Elemental,     living flame,  Destruction unleashed,     wild and never tame.  Causing all it touches     then to burn,  Leaving only ashes     in return.

column1: The Fire Elemental is created by a spell cast by a User of Magic.  Once created, it burns and destroys, uncontrolled by any master.
column2: The Fire Elemental is created by a spell cast by a User of Magic.  Once created, it burns and destroys, uncontrolled by any master.

column1: A thing of water,     living contradiction,  Despite the waters     flowing benediction,  For in its power     at its very worst  Forces men to suffer,     die of thirst.
column2: A thing of water,     living contradiction,  Despite the waters     flowing benediction,  For in its power     at its very worst  Forces men to suffer,     die of thirst.

column1: The Wind which turns and twists     with speeding force,  It wrecks and ruins all     without remorse.  The dust of devils,     Vortex of despair,  A living Elemental     formed of Air.
column2: The Wind which turns and twists     with speeding force,  It wrecks and ruins all     without remorse.  The dust of devils,     Vortex of despair,  A living Elemental     formed of Air.

column1: The might of Earth     and Strength of Rock transformed,  And through the land     the creature rose and stormed.  The shape of man     was made by force of spell  Until the fire's fury     shall it quell.
column2: The might of Earth     and Strength of Rock transformed,  And through the land     the creature rose and stormed.  The shape of man     was made by force of spell  Until the fire's fury     shall it quell.

column1: Thing of magic shaped     by Magic Spell,  Unleashed upon the land     by Wizard fell.  Half-life thing that hates     all things alive,  Chaos loosed, and in     destruction thrive.
column2: Thing of magic shaped     by Magic Spell,  Unleashed upon the land     by Wizard fell.  Half-life thing that hates     all things alive,  Chaos loosed, and in     destruction thrive.

column1: A Monster is a thing     which all must find,  And that which is most fright'ning,     hides within the mind  All must face this shadow     to give to it a name,  For without the knowing,     the thing will but remain.
column2: A Monster is a thing     which all must find,  And that which is most fright'ning,     hides within the mind  All must face this shadow     to give to it a name,  For without the knowing,     the thing will but remain.

column1: Monster is a word for many things, unless one knows what it truly is, it cannot be overcome.
column2: Monster is a word for many things, unless one knows what it truly is, it cannot be overcome.

column1: Learn ye now the magic     of this land,  To summon up the djinn     and give command,  To force your very foe     to be a friend  To turn a man to beast     and beast to man.
column2: Learn ye now the magic     of this land,  To summon up the djinn     and give command,  To force your very foe     to be a friend  To turn a man to beast     and beast to man.

column1: There is a Sultan     in the land Shapeir  Who speaks but truth and     counsels all to hear,  To whom all the people     praise and cheer,  The righteous rejoice,     and the foul all fear.
column2: There is a Sultan     in the land Shapeir  Who speaks but truth and     counsels all to hear,  To whom all the people     praise and cheer,  The righteous rejoice,     and the foul all fear.

column1: A tale is told of     this same time last year,  That Magic caused     the man to disappear.  A brother now is     ruling in Raseir,  And no one knows the     fate of the Emir.
column2: A tale is told of     this same time last year,  That Magic caused     the man to disappear.  A brother now is     ruling in Raseir,  And no one knows the     fate of the Emir.

column1: A Wizard once did     open up a door  Unto a place that     none had seen before,  Where spells are taught     and magic is the key  To ponder over     dusty books of lore.
column2: A Wizard once did     open up a door  Unto a place that     none had seen before,  Where spells are taught     and magic is the key  To ponder over     dusty books of lore.

column1: The Palace gates are open     to but few  Only those the Sultan summons     can go through.
column2: The Palace gates are open     to but few  Only those the Sultan summons     can go through.

column1: To journey far across     this mighty land,  To face the deadly driving     dusts of sand,  The shining sun which sears     the naked hand,  And to survive, it must     be caravanned.
column2: To journey far across     this mighty land,  To face the deadly driving     dusts of sand,  The shining sun which sears     the naked hand,  And to survive, it must     be caravanned.

column1: A place where all must fight     just to survive  Forethought and wit shall help     thee stay alive.
column2: A place where all must fight     just to survive  Forethought and wit shall help     thee stay alive.

column1: Some questions are far     better left unsaid  For greater troubles     follow in their stead.
column2: Some questions are far     better left unsaid  For greater troubles     follow in their stead.

column1: A place where the adventurer     may pause,  And practice for a while,     or choose a cause.  No matter where he travels,     there he knows  He will find the Guild Hall waiting     where he goes.
column2: A place where the adventurer     may pause,  And practice for a while,     or choose a cause.  No matter where he travels,     there he knows  He will find the Guild Hall waiting     where he goes.

column1: It is said that in Shapeir     there is an Inn  That in all the world     you will not find its twin.  The food is fine when     there you care to dine  Most excellently     run by Katta kin.
column2: It is said that in Shapeir     there is an Inn  That in all the world     you will not find its twin.  The food is fine when     there you care to dine  Most excellently     run by Katta kin.

column1: In the desert in the day     it's always hot,  And then again at night     it's always not.
column2: In the desert in the day     it's always hot,  And then again at night     it's always not.

column1: In the center of the city     stands the fountain flowing free,  Pours the lifeblood of the living:     water washing endlessly.
column2: In the center of the city     stands the fountain flowing free,  Pours the lifeblood of the living:     water washing endlessly.

column1: It is whispered     by the Katta  That a hero     had been sought.  By their kindred     seeking elsewhere  To this city     he was brought.
column2: It is whispered     by the Katta  That a hero     had been sought.  By their kindred     seeking elsewhere  To this city     he was brought.

column1: From their homeland     they were banished;  Now they seek there     to return  And their hero     shall return them  To the place     they ever yearn.
column2: From their homeland     they were banished;  Now they seek there     to return  And their hero     shall return them  To the place     they ever yearn.

column1: When a stranger seeks     to purchase in this land,  Some things of finery     or of purpose grand,  From the shops of the bazaar,     he must first have a dinar,  The Money Changer's purse     will fill his hand.
column2: When a stranger seeks     to purchase in this land,  Some things of finery     or of purpose grand,  From the shops of the bazaar,     he must first have a dinar,  The Money Changer's purse     will fill his hand.

column1: The Money Changer exchanges coins from distant lands to our own centimes and dinars.
column2: The Money Changer exchanges coins from distant lands to our own centimes and dinars.

column1: Through the door at the end     of the Tarik of Stars, is a man  Who can see in the lights     of the sky in the night nature's plan  For the men on the sands     and the towns of the lands down below  What is writ of the date     and the deeds that their fate will bestow.
column2: Through the door at the end     of the Tarik of Stars, is a man  Who can see in the lights     of the sky in the night nature's plan  For the men on the sands     and the towns of the lands down below  What is writ of the date     and the deeds that their fate will bestow.

column1: A Wizard Woman weaves     the warp of spells,  By which is watched     what incidents befell,  Aziza sees the present     and the past,  Perceives what is,     and thus, shall be, foretells.
column2: A Wizard Woman weaves     the warp of spells,  By which is watched     what incidents befell,  Aziza sees the present     and the past,  Perceives what is,     and thus, shall be, foretells.

column1: I am the Poet, teller of tales     that all might hear,  Speaker of Speeches, Weaver of words     for all Shapeir,  Giver of gossip, Writer of rhymes,     Narrator of News,  I am called Omar, Teacher of truths     to all now near.
column2: I am the Poet, teller of tales     that all might hear,  Speaker of Speeches, Weaver of words     for all Shapeir,  Giver of gossip, Writer of rhymes,     Narrator of News,  I am called Omar, Teacher of truths     to all now near.

column1: For all who journey from     the lands afar,  The center of the city,     the bazaar,  is filled with merchants' magic,     and shall be,  A wonder to behold,     a joy to see.
column2: For all who journey from     the lands afar,  The center of the city,     the bazaar,  is filled with merchants' magic,     and shall be,  A wonder to behold,     a joy to see.

column1: The Dervish of the desert     sits upon the sands beside the pool,  Of the Oasis as men pass him     and they laugh in ridicule at such a fool,  But he watches and he listens     and he thinks upon the things which pass him by,  And he smiles upon his mockers     when they question all his answers in reply.
column2: The Dervish of the desert     sits upon the sands beside the pool,  Of the Oasis as men pass him     and they laugh in ridicule at such a fool,  But he watches and he listens     and he thinks upon the things which pass him by,  And he smiles upon his mockers     when they question all his answers in reply.

column1: A poem is but words     used in a different way,  In which the meaning is     far more than words can say.
column2: A poem is but words     used in a different way,  In which the meaning is     far more than words can say.

column1: A poem goes beyond the mere meaning of its words to express the deeper truths within it.
column2: A poem goes beyond the mere meaning of its words to express the deeper truths within it.

column1: You applaud loudly and long for your friend Shema's dancing prowess.
column2: You applaud loudly and long for your friend Shema's dancing prowess.

column1: You receive a reward of 50 Dinars.
column2: You receive a reward of 50 Dinars.

column1: You receive a reward of 100 Dinars.
column2: You receive a reward of 100 Dinars.

column1: Is it not amazing how quickly we have set up this Inn?   The Katta's Tail is already the center of Shapeir's entertainment.  Where else can you watch Shema dance?
column2: Is it not amazing how quickly we have set up this Inn?   The Katta's Tail is already the center of Shapeir's entertainment.  Where else can you watch Shema dance?

column1: It is good to be back home, even though I have had troubling news.  My old friend Arus Al-Din, the Emir of Raseir, is missing.  He was a good man, but not as wise as I.
column2: It is good to be back home, even though I have had troubling news.  My old friend Arus Al-Din, the Emir of Raseir, is missing.  He was a good man, but not as wise as I.

column1: Good Day, my fine friend.  I trust you are finding your stay in Shapeir interesting?  There is always so much to see and do here.
column2: Good Day, my fine friend.  I trust you are finding your stay in Shapeir interesting?  There is always so much to see and do here.

column1: Only a true Hero could have defeated the dreadful Fire Elemental.
column2: Only a true Hero could have defeated the dreadful Fire Elemental.

column1: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Djinn A shadow passed over the Katta's Tail Inn Astrologers foretell that Doom shall come to dwell And Shapeir shall be but sand upon the wind.
column2: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Djinn A shadow passed over the Katta's Tail Inn Astrologers foretell that Doom shall come to dwell And Shapeir shall be but sand upon the wind.

column1: Comes a hero from the North,     riding on the very air, And this is sign the first to then beware, For Darkness soon shall fall and shadow cover all, The city and the ones now living there.
column2: Comes a hero from the North,     riding on the very air, And this is sign the first to then beware, For Darkness soon shall fall and shadow cover all, The city and the ones now living there.
